content.text:
Now I have a very clear view of atom C:6 and all its covalent bonds. From the zoomed-in views, I can identify the following neighbour atoms of **C:6**:

## Neighbour Atoms of C:6

| Atomic Index | Element |
|---|---|
| 1 | C |
| 5 | C |
| 7 | N |
| 8 | C |

**Summary:**
- **C:1** — part of the aromatic ring above
- **C:5** — part of the aromatic ring to the left
- **N:7** — nitrogen atom below/right (part of a 5-membered ring)
- **C:8** — carbon atom below (also part of the 5-membered ring, connected through N:7 path and directly bonded to C:6)

Atom C:6 sits at a ring junction, connecting the 6-membered aromatic ring (with C:1, C:2, C:3, C:4, C:5) and a 5-membered ring (with N:7, C:8).

**geomconf_index: 3649**

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 6, whose element should be C. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
